sliderImages.title: Cook Together
sliderImages.subtitle: Share the joy of cooking with friends

recipes.title: Avocado Toast with Poached Egg
instructions: Toast the sourdough slices until golden and crispy on both sides.
Halve the avocado, remove the pit, and scoop the flesh into a bowl. Add lemon juice, salt, and pepper, then mash to your preferred consistency.
Bring a pot of water to a gentle simmer. Add the white vinegar. Crack each egg into a small cup.
Create a gentle swirl in the water with a spoon, then slide in the eggs one at a time. Poach for 3 minutes for a runny yolk.
Spread the mashed avocado generously over each toast slice.
Lift the poached eggs with a slotted spoon, pat dry, and place on top of the avocado.
Season with chilli flakes, salt, and pepper. Garnish with micro herbs if using. Serve immediately.

recipes.title: Fluffy Banana Pancakes
instructions: Mash the bananas in a large bowl until smooth.
Whisk in the eggs and buttermilk until well combined.
Sift in the flour, baking powder, baking soda, sugar, and salt. Fold gently until just combined — a few lumps are fine.
Heat a non-stick pan over medium heat and add a small knob of butter.
Pour about 1/4 cup of batter per pancake. Cook until bubbles form on the surface and edges look set, about 2–3 minutes.
Flip and cook for another 1–2 minutes until golden.
Stack and serve warm with maple syrup and sliced bananas.

recipes.title: Classic Greek Salad
instructions: Combine the tomatoes, cucumber, red onion, bell pepper, and olives in a large bowl.
Whisk together the olive oil, red wine vinegar, oregano, salt, and pepper to make the dressing.
Pour the dressing over the salad and toss gently to coat all the vegetables.
Top with the feta cheese cubes — don't crumble them, leave them in chunks.
Finish with an extra drizzle of olive oil and a pinch of dried oregano.
Serve immediately with crusty bread.

recipes.title: Grilled Chicken Caesar Wrap
instructions: Season chicken breasts with garlic powder, paprika, salt, pepper, and olive oil.
Grill the chicken on a hot griddle pan for 6–7 minutes per side until cooked through. Let rest for 5 minutes.
Slice the chicken into thin strips.
Toss romaine lettuce with Caesar dressing and Parmesan.
Warm the tortillas in a dry pan for 30 seconds each side.
Lay the lettuce mix in the centre of each tortilla, top with chicken strips.
Roll tightly, folding in the sides, and slice diagonally. Serve immediately.

recipes.title: Spaghetti Carbonara
instructions: Bring a large pot of well-salted water to a boil. Cook spaghetti until al dente.
Meanwhile, fry the guanciale in a large pan over medium heat until crispy. Add garlic for the last minute, then discard the garlic.
In a bowl, whisk together eggs, Pecorino, and Parmesan. Season generously with black pepper.
Reserve 1 cup of pasta cooking water, then drain the spaghetti.
Remove the pan with guanciale from heat. Add the hot spaghetti and toss to coat in the fat.
Let the pan cool slightly, then pour over the egg mixture. Toss vigorously, adding pasta water a splash at a time to create a creamy, glossy sauce.
Serve immediately with extra cheese and a generous crack of black pepper.

recipes.title: Butter Chicken
instructions: Marinate chicken in yogurt, 1 tsp garam masala, and turmeric for at least 30 minutes.
Cook chicken in a hot pan until slightly charred. Set aside.
In the same pan, melt butter over medium heat. Sauté onion until golden, about 8 minutes.
Add garlic, ginger, cumin, coriander, and chilli powder. Cook for 2 minutes until fragrant.
Pour in crushed tomatoes and simmer for 10 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Blend the sauce until smooth. Return to pan and stir in cream and remaining garam masala.
Add the cooked chicken and simmer for 10 minutes. Adjust seasoning.
Garnish with fresh coriander and serve with naan or basmati rice.

recipes.title: Chocolate Lava Cake
instructions: Preheat oven to 220°C / 425°F. Butter four ramekins and dust with cocoa powder.
Melt chocolate and butter together in a heatproof bowl over simmering water. Stir until smooth. Let cool slightly.
Whisk eggs, yolks, and icing sugar until pale and slightly thickened.
Fold the chocolate mixture into the egg mixture gently.
Sift in the flour and salt, and fold until just combined.
Divide batter evenly among the prepared ramekins. Chill for at least 15 minutes (or up to 24 hours).
Bake for exactly 12 minutes — the edges should be set but the centre still jiggly.
Invert onto plates immediately and serve with vanilla ice cream.

recipes.title: Tropical Mango Smoothie
instructions: Add mango cubes to a blender along with the ice cubes.
Pour in the coconut milk and add the Greek yogurt.
Add honey, grated ginger, and lime juice.
Blend on high for 60 seconds until completely smooth and creamy.
Taste and adjust sweetness with more honey if needed.
Pour into chilled glasses and garnish with fresh mint leaves.
Serve immediately for best flavour.

recipes.title: Fresh Guacamole & Chips
instructions: Halve and pit the avocados. Scoop the flesh into a bowl.
Add lime juice and salt. Mash with a fork to your desired consistency — chunky or smooth.
Fold in the red onion, tomatoes, cilantro, jalapeño, and garlic.
Taste and adjust seasoning — add more salt or lime as needed.
Cover with plastic wrap pressed directly onto the surface to prevent browning.
Serve immediately with tortilla chips, or refrigerate for up to 2 hours.
